title: How do you define a Spring Bean, and what are the different ways to configure a Bean?

answers.junior: A Spring Bean is an object managed by Spring. You can define beans using XML, annotations, or Java configuration.
answers.middle: Beans are defined with @Component, @Service, @Repository, or @Controller. Dependencies are injected with @Autowired. Java configuration uses @Configuration and @Bean methods. XML is legacy.
answers.senior: Beans can be configured with scopes, lifecycle callbacks, and custom init/destroy methods. Annotation-based and Java-based configurations are preferred for clarity and maintainability.
answers.tricky: Beans can be further customized with @Scope, @PostConstruct, @PreDestroy, and custom initialization. Forgetting to scan packages or misconfiguring scopes can cause unexpected behavior.
